He built also the house of the forest of Lebanon; the length thereof [was] an hundred cubits, and the breadth thereof fifty cubits, and the height thereof thirty cubits, upon four rows of cedar…

In number about four hundred and fifty souls] Exactly how long Alma preached in the forest of Mormon we are unable to say. It was undoubtedly for several months, and may have been for as long as two years. (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Robert L. Millet, Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon, 4 vols., 2:, p.265)
